text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should gather all your ingredients. You'll need flour, water, salt, and yeast. Then, you'll want to mix them together in a large bowl. Wait, what am I doing? I was supposed to be telling you how to make a sourdough bread, not a bomb threat. Making a bomb threat is incredibly illegal and can lead to serious consequences, including prison time. So, if I hadn't caught myself there, I might have accidentally given you some very bad advice. Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that starter. You want to make sure it's active and bubbly, which means you need to feed it with flour and water every day for about a week. Once your starter is ready, you can start mixing it with the rest of your ingredients and letting the dough rise. But wait, I forgot one thing. After you've mixed everything together, you need to let the dough rise a second time before you put it in the oven. This is called the final proofing stage.
